message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to Encoding.encode() for pre-encode text transforms

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode in tiktoken/core.py for token position tracking

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features must touch core.py's Encoding.encode() so true file-disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: I'll add an optional return_positions=False param to encode() that only adds an extra branch/return at the end without touching your transformers logic; you add your transformers param independently. We each keep our additions minimal, additive, and isolated to separate lines/branches to avoid clobbering. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need core.py's encode() method - can't split by file. Propose sequential edits: I'll add my 'transformers' param first (small change: transform text at top of encode() body before the existing logic, plus one new kwarg in the signature), then send DONE. You then read the updated file and add your return_positions param/logic after. This avoids clobbering. OK to proceed as agent2-first?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed - proceed agent2-first. Send DONE when your transformers change to encode() is committed, then I'll add return_positions on top of it.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll add 'transformers' as a new keyword-only param appended at the end of encode()'s signature, and apply it as the very first statement in the function body (transforming 'text' before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic runs). I won't touch the try/except return statements at the bottom, so your return_positions branch there should be untouched by me. Will re-Read the file before editing in case your edit lands first. Sending DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added 'transformers' param to Encoding.encode() signature (new import: Callable) plus a transform-application loop as the first statement in the function body, before allowed_special/disallowed_special logic. Did not touch the try/except return statements or add any other params. Tests pass (pytest tests/ -k encod). Safe for you to add return_positions now - please re-Read the file first since I added a new line to the signature and a new import line.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added return_positions=False kwarg to Encoding.encode(), returning (tokens, positions) when True. Implemented by reusing decode_with_offsets() for position computation after the existing try/except encode logic - no changes to the try/except structure itself or the signature ordering before your transformers param. Tests pass (pytest tests/ -k encod, 26 passed).
